item.title: Automated Weak-to-Strong Researcher
item.description: We built autonomous AI agents that propose ideas, run experiments, and iterate on an open research problem—how to train a strong model using only a weaker model's supervision—and found they outperform human researchers, suggesting that automating this kind of

item.title: Abstractive Red-Teaming of Language Model Character
item.description: How can we surface realistic failures of model character prior to deployment? We introduce abstractive red-teaming, which searches for natural-language categories of user queries that reliably elicit character violations.

item.title: Measuring and improving coding audit realism with deployment resources
item.description: We study realism win rate, a metric for measuring how distinguishable Petri audit transcripts are from real deployment interactions, and use it to evaluate the effect of giving the auditor real deployment resources.

item.title: A3: An Automated Alignment Agent for Safety Finetuning
item.description: We introduce our Automated Alignment Agent (A3), a new agentic framework which automatically mitigates safety failures in Large Language Models with minimal human intervention.

item.title: AuditBench: Evaluating Alignment Auditing Techniques on Models with Hidden Behaviors
item.description: We release AuditBench, a benchmark of 56 language models with implanted hidden behaviors for evaluating progress in alignment auditing.

item.title: 3 Challenges and 2 Hopes for the Safety of Unsupervised Elicitation
item.description: We stress-test unsupervised elicitation and easy-to-hard techniques on new datasets meant to capture realistic challenges such techniques would likely face.

item.title: The Persona Selection Model: Why AI Assistants might Behave like Humans
item.description: We discuss a perspective where Claude is something like a character in an AI-generated story.

item.title: The Hot Mess of AI: How Does Misalignment Scale with Model Intelligence and Task Complexity?
item.description: When AI systems fail, will they fail by systematically pursuing goals we do not intend? Or will they fail by being a hot mess—taking nonsensical actions that do not further any goal?

item.title: Pre-deployment auditing can catch an overt saboteur
item.description: We test whether our pre-deployment alignment auditing methods can catch models trained to overtly sabotage Anthropic.

item.title: Petri 2.0: New Scenarios, New Model Comparisons, and Improved Eval-Awareness Mitigations
item.description: We've improved our Petri automated-behavioral-auditing tool with improved realism mitigations to counter eval-awareness, an expanded seed library with 70 new scenarios, and evaluation results for more recent frontier models.

item.title: Bloom: an open source tool for automated behavioral evaluations
item.description: Bloom is an open-source automated pipeline that generates configurable evaluation suites to measure arbitrary behavioral traits in frontier LLMs without requiring ground-truth labels.

item.title: Activation Oracles: Training and Evaluating LLMs as General-Purpose Activation Explainers
item.description: We train language models to answer questions about their own activations in natural language and evaluate how well they generalize to settings very unlike their training, such as uncovering misalignment introduced during fine-tuning.

item.title: Towards training-time mitigations for alignment faking in RL
item.description: We construct a diverse array of model organisms of alignment faking and study mitigations that could be used during RL to decrease alignment faking and compliance gaps.

item.title: Open Source Replication of the Auditing Game Model Organism
item.description: We release an open source replication of the model organism from our previous auditing game paper.

item.title: Anthropic Fellows Program 2026
item.description: Apply now for our AI safety research fellowship.

item.title: Beyond Data Filtering: Knowledge Localization for Capability Removal in LLMs
item.description: We localize dangerous knowledge to a subset of model's parameters, so it can be easily removed after training.

item.title: Evaluating honesty and lie detection techniques on a diverse suite dishonest models
item.description: We explore techniques for honesty elicitation and lie detection on a diverse testbed of dishonest model organisms.

item.title: Strengthening Red Teams: A Modular Scaffold for Control Evaluations
item.description: We decompose sabotage into constituent skills and use synthetic simulations to strengthen attacks in complex environments.

item.title: Anthropic's Pilot Sabotage Risk Report
item.description: We release a report on the level of risk posed by our deployed models from emerging forms of misalignment, as of Summer 2025. We conclude that the level of risk is very low but not fully negligible.

item.title: Stress-testing model specs reveals character differences among language models
item.description: We generated 300,000+ queries testing value trade-offs in AI models from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and xAI. Each model showed distinct value prioritization patterns, and we found thousands of cases of direct co
